mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods found in this batch. Batch 7 contains core utilities, validation, and dashboard helpers, but no printer enrollment or subscription setup APIs. These components likely exist in enrollment helper modules or API helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription state verification methods found in this batch. Methods to check or set subscription properties (like free months) would be in subscription management or API helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. Status checking methods would be in subscription API helpers or state management modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Navigates to the dashboard portal and handles login if needed. This is the entry point for accessing the dashboard where pause plan functionality would be available.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method provides navigation to the dashboard where the Pause Instant Ink link would be located. It's the first step needed to access pause plan functionality, though the actual pause plan clicking would require additional page object methods not in this batch.

precondition_mapping.notes: Found dashboard access method which is needed to reach the pause plan functionality, but the actual pause plan button/link clicking methods are not in this batch. The CancellationPlanHelper in this batch is for cancellation flows, not pause plan. Pause plan page objects and action methods likely exist in other batches.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Navigates to the dashboard portal and handles login if needed. Waits for page load, navigates to portal shell URL, handles login if on login page, and verifies dashboard access by checking Instant Ink menu link visibility.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 require navigating to Print and Payment History page, which is under HP Instant Ink section. This method provides the dashboard access needed before navigating to specific pages. Also relevant to precondition 3 for accessing dashboard to trigger pause plan.

matched_methods.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Main validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Handles screenshot-only mode, full element validation, image comparison with exclusions, and animation validation.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Multiple steps require verification of UI elements, text values, progress bars, and page state. This is the core validation function that can be used at each checkpoint to verify expected results by comparing against baseline data.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel image comparison with exclusion region filtering and bounding box generation for failures. Compares two images, filters out pixels in exclusion regions, generates suggested exclusion boxes for failures, and returns pass/fail status with detailed failure information.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 11 and 13 require verifying progress bar visual state (color fill, updated values) after page refresh. This method can compare screenshots of the progress bars before and after to verify visual changes.

matched_methods.method_signature: def save_full_page_screenshot(page: Page, flow_name: str, context_name: str, output_dir: str = None) -> str
matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot and saves it with a timestamped filename. Creates output directory if needed, generates sanitized filename with timestamp, captures full scrollable page, and returns the filename.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 require verifying page loads successfully. Step 20 explicitly requires visual verification via screenshot. This method captures full-page screenshots for validation checkpoints.

matched_methods.method_signature: def take_element_screenshot(page: Page, selector: str, output_path: str) -> bool
matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element on the page identified by a selector and saves it to the specified path. Checks element visibility, creates output directory, captures element screenshot, and returns success status.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Multiple steps require capturing and verifying specific UI elements like billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, and value displays. This method captures element-specific screenshots for detailed verification.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts the visible inner text from a Playwright element and returns it as a trimmed string. Gets user-visible text respecting CSS visibility, strips whitespace.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 8, 9, 11, 14, 17, 18, 19 require reading and verifying text values from UI elements (complimentary pages value, additional pages value, info messages, total pages). This method extracts text for comparison.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Checks if a Playwright element is hidden or disabled by evaluating JavaScript in the browser context. Checks offsetParent for hidden elements and disabled property for form elements.
matched_methods.usage_example: is_hidden = is_element_hidden_or_disabled(element_handle)  # Returns True if hidden or disabled
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 2 requires verifying plan pause information is NOT displayed (hidden). Step 5 requires verifying it IS displayed (not hidden). This method checks element visibility state.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves the position and dimensions of a Playwright element as a dictionary. Gets bounding box with x, y, width, height, returns dict with integer values, handles invisible elements gracefully.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 6, 13, 14 involve verifying progress bars are displayed and properly positioned. This method gets element position and dimensions for layout verification.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts computed CSS styles from a Playwright element by executing JavaScript in the browser context. Returns dict with font_family, font_size, font_weight, color, background, text_align properties.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 11 and 14 require verifying progress bar colors (black for complimentary, yellow for additional). This method extracts CSS styles including background color for verification.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Generates and returns the current timestamp as a formatted string in the pattern YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS. Uses datetime.now() and strftime for formatting.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 3 involves time manipulation (32-day shift). While this method doesn't perform time shifting, it provides timestamp generation which may be useful for logging the time shift operation or generating unique identifiers for test runs.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Sanitizes a filename string by replacing all invalid filesystem characters with underscores. Uses regex to replace backslash, forward slash, asterisk, question mark, colon, double quote, less than, greater than, and pipe characters.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 11, 13, 20 involve capturing screenshots with generated filenames. This utility ensures screenshot filenames are filesystem-safe by removing invalid characters.

matched_methods.method_signature: def run_flow(mode: str, flow_name: str, flow_args: Optional[Dict] = None, context_override: Optional[str] = None) -> None
matched_methods.what_it_does: Main orchestration function that executes a test flow in scrape, validate, or noop mode with comprehensive setup, execution, and reporting. Dynamically imports flow modules, sets up output directories, initializes configuration, executes flow with callbacks, handles exceptions, and finalizes reports.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This is the main flow execution engine for the entire framework. It would orchestrate the execution of the billing cycle period card test flow, handling setup, execution, validation, and reporting.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Comprehensive static helper class providing utility methods for all dashboard-related test operations including dashboard access, navigation, first-time setup, status card verification, plan management, billing operations, payment handling, printer selection, and various dashboard UI interactions.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides dashboard access methods needed to navigate to Print and Payment History page (steps 1, 4) and to access pause plan functionality (precondition 3). Contains 70+ methods for comprehensive dashboard testing.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. Contains methods for selecting cancellation feedback, verifying cancellation UI elements, validating cancellation timeline, handling plan changes, and managing subscription restoration.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: While this helper is for cancellation flows (not pause plan), it demonstrates the pattern for subscription management helpers. The actual pause plan helper would likely be in a different batch. This helper shows how subscription state changes are tested in the framework.

matched_constants.relevance_reason: Steps 11, 13, 14, 18, 20 involve visual verification of progress bars and screenshots. This constant defines the color tolerance threshold for image comparison, allowing for minor rendering variations while detecting significant visual differences.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: Batch 7 provides partial coverage for test case C44873414. Found comprehensive validation and verification utilities in core/validator.py and core/utils.py including screenshot capture, image comparison, text extraction, style extraction, and element visibility checking. Found dashboard access methods in dashboard_helper.py which provide navigation to the dashboard where Print and Payment History page would be accessed. However, this batch lacks: (1) Page objects for Print and Payment History page with specific element locators (billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, value displays), (2) API helpers for printer enrollment, subscription management, time manipulation, and print job simulation, (3) Navigation methods to Print and Payment History page specifically, (4) Pause plan trigger methods and page objects, (5) Device-specific interaction utilities (hover vs click), (6) Viewport resizing for responsive testing. The validation and verification methods found are highly reusable across all verification steps. The missing components are likely in other batches containing page objects, API helpers, and enrollment utilities. This batch provides the validation infrastructure but not the test-specific page objects and data setup methods.
